Name: Quick Hummus
Cuisine: Lebanese
Type: Dip
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking Chole/Chickpeas)
Cook TIme: 5 mins
Spice Level: 3/5
Savoury Level: 4/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 4/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from chole
Main Flavour profile: Rich Earthy flavour and Garlic dominant

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Garlic Cloves 4-5
Oregano 1-1.5 tbsp
Chilli Flakes 1-1.5 tbsp
Salt 
Olive Oil 4-8 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Boil the chole in a pressure cooker
Take the boiled chole and add it into nutri blender
Add in Garlic cloves, 2-4 tbsp olive oid, oregano and chilli flakes, pinch of salt
Might need to add water 2-4 tbsp 
Mix till smooth
Store in jar, drizzle ½ tbsp of olive oil 

My Notes: This is my quick hummus, infact preferred hummus recipe as it is more savoury and garlic, olive oil naturally shine through. Adding in the oregano can make it more italian but as a matter of fact the earth taste of chickpeas and garlic is so strong that the oregano does nothing but rather just alleviates their flavour. The only possible flag is that the creaminess is not as good as traditional hummus so to counter that I add in a little water to make it more smoother. The Chilli flakes help in additional spice

Alternatives:
Add Chopped Olives to make it richer

PERSONAL RECIPE:
Name: Dal Tadka
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 30mins
Cook TIme: 15mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Onion 1 piece 
Tomato 1 piece
Green Chilli 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon)
Dried Red chillies 4-5 piece 
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add them into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add tomatoes
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: The amount of garlic is high and is cooked for a longer period as the burnt garlic brings out the smokey flavour of the dal that perfectly pairs with the high amount of spice. The sweetness comes from the onions as I let the carmelise a little longer till they are brown as it brings out the natruat oniony sweetness without the need of adding sugar. I like my dal on the thicker side but not too thick its should be soupy yet slurpy, essentially should be able to bite down on few of the lentils.
Alternatives:
Channa Dal 
Urid Dal
Moong Dal 

Additional Options:
Adding Spinach in the pressure cooker with dal will make a new dish called dal palak

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name: Masala Khicdi 
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 20mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Rice 1 Cup
Onion 1 to 1.5 piece 
Tomato 1 to 1.5 piece
Capsicum ½ piece
Carrot ½ piece
Peas ¼ cup
Green beans ¼ cup
Potatoes 1
Corn ¼ cup
Green Chillie 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon, Khichdi Masala 1 tsp)
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Onions should be finely diced cut
Carots, green beans, potatoes can be diced cut or kept long
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add dal and rice together into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder and corn
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add rest of the vegies
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal and rice in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: I like to add more garlic to cook it a little longer than required to enure its taste is prevalent in the dish as a lot of different spices are being added. The green chilli helps in giving the dish natural spice. I love adding more vegetables in every dish of mine, the make the dish very fresh and fibrous. The sweetness or hint of sweetness comes from carrots, peas and potatoes which is more than enough for this dish, the carmelised onions also add to the sweetness. Also I like my khichdi being soft and mushy.

Alternatives:
In vegetables anything can be used but onion tomatoes and potatoes are a must
Green Moong Dal instead of toor dal

Additional Options:
Spinach 
Cauliflower 
For more protein add in boiled soya chunks about ¼ cup

Name: One Pot Chole/chickpea Curry
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking chole/chickpeas) + 20 mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from onions
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky, earthy taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Onions 2-3 pieces
Tomatoes 1-2 pieces
Garlic Cloves 6-7
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric ½  teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Black Peppercons ½ teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1.5 teaspoon, Chole Masala 1.5 tsp, Kitchen King Masala 1 tsp)
Dried Red Chilli 4-5 pieces
Bay leaves 2-3 pieces
Tea 1 tsp
Salt 2-3 tsp
Kastoori Methi ½ tablespoon
Oil 3-5 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Soak Chole/chickpeas in water overnight or 8 hours
In pressure cooker ad 3-5 tbsp of oil and let it heat
Then add peppercorns, chopped garlic cloves, dried red chillies, bay leaves, cummin seeds and saute until garlic is brown in color
Add chopped onions (juillien of half moons), now let them saute till they are dark brown
Add in the spices and mix well, the base should look deep dark brown
Then add diced tomatoes and mix will
Add the soaked chickpeas and mix well with the curry base
Add water a little over the point till the chole are submerged
Optional Step: Before adding water boil it with tea and then add the water to the pressure cooker
Let it cook for 5-6 whistles
Add in the crushed kastoori methi and sqeed half a lemon
Serve it


My Notes: The main reason behind adding tea water to cook chole is to give it a high umami taste and black color, but I can achieve it without it. What I essentially do is really caremalize my onions and garlic till they are dark brown and then mix it with the spices. Due to this step the base of the curry already looks brown and the burnt onions give it a great umami and sweet taste to the curry, whilst the garlic balances all the spices. Due to this step one can skip the tea part, although I still add the tea as it elevates the entire dish to a whole different level of spice, umami and savoury flavours.

Alternatives:
No dried red chilli → extra red chilli powder, but add it later so it doesn't burn
No kasuri methi → skip it, nothing replicates it exactly but dried fenugreek seeds (half the quantity) get close
Want it more tangy → extra half lemon or a spoon of tamarind paste with the tomatoes 
Want it creamier → spoon of yoghurt stirred in after the last whistle

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name:  No Bake Strawberry Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Strawberry and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich, Creamy, fruity, slightly tangy taste overall sweet

Ingredients:
Digestive Biscuit 1 packet
Strawberries 1 pack
Brown sugar 4 tbps
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly also add diced up strawberries, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. The main flavour profile is strawberries, it pairs perfectly with whipping cream and cream cheese. The tine chunks in the main base lifts the dist to a new level. The compote does have added sugar but it is very less than what is usually recommended

Alternatives:
Can use ready made strawberry sauce but wont recommend

***

**Alternatives for strawberry:**
Blueberries, raspberries, or blackberries. Use similar weights and maintain the compote process.

Name:  Mango paneer Ice cream 
Cuisine: Ice Cream
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 10 mins 
Cook TIme: 8 hours
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Mango
Main Flavour profile: Fruity, sweet and mango 

Ingredients:
Paneer 200g
Milk ¼ cup
Natural Mango pulp 200g
Diced Mango ⅛ cup

Procedure:
Blend paneer, milk, mango pulp in nutri blender
Ensure it is completely smooth
Pour it in a jar, and top it with diced mango
Take a natural flower petal and place in center
Let it set in the fridge for 8 hours

My Notes: It actually tastes like ice cream, smooth creamy and sweet. But it has no added sugar, naturally made and is high in protein a perfect dessert when on a diet cut. The natural mango pulp contributes to its natural sweetness and tanginess. The diced mango are fun to chew on every bite. The flower petal in the middle is my signature move take it out before eating just for aethetics.

Alternatives:
Can use store mango juice 
Can add dry fruits such as raisins, almonds, cashews and pistachios
Name:  Banana Bread
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 20 mins 
Cook TIme: 50 mins
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Banana
Main Flavour profile: Naturally sweet with backend chocolatey taste

Ingredients:
Ripe Bananas 2 pieces
Dark chocolate chunks ¼ cup
Baking soda 1 tsp
Vanilla extract few drops 
Yogurt ⅓ cup
Whole wheat flour 1 cup
Brown sugar 4 tbsp
Almonds 5 chopped 

Procedure:
Mash the ripe bananas thoroughly, add yogurt and vanilla extract and mix well
Add the flour and sugar in the mixture and mix well
Add in dark chocolate chunks
Transfer in cake tinm top it up with more chocolate chunks and almonds
Bake at 180 degrees for 50 mins

My Notes: Natural sweetness from the banana dominates and pairs perfectly dark chocolate as they mitigare the overt sweetness. The yogurt makes the cake super moist and fluffym and it is low sugar and healthy as it uses whole wheat and not all purpose flour.

Alternatives:
Can add more dry fruits

Name:  No Bake Biscoff Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Biscoff and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Sweet, slight saltiness, biscoff

Ingredients:
Biscoff Biscuit 1 packet
Biscoff Spread ½ Jar
Whipping Cream ¼ cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add Biscoff spread and mix it well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt remaining biscoff spread and pour it over the filling
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, cheese, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. Biscoff biscuit gives out rich carmelised butter taste the crunchy base that is, the center filling is smooth, creamy and sweet topped with pure melted biscoff spread creating the perfect dessert.
 
Alternatives:
None, the recipe is full proof

Ingredients:

75% Bitter Chocolate ¾ Bar
55% Dark Chocolate ¾ Bar
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add melted 55% Dark chocolate and mix well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt the 75% bitter chocolate and let it cool a bit, then add remaining whipping cream and mix well.
Pour the bitter chocolate ganache on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, chocolatey, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. I love bitter chocolate and overall dark chocolates. The main flavour profile is for dark chocolate, the base oreos althoug sweet the biscuit is slightly bitter, then we increase the bitterness of the chocolate in the main filling by adding dark chocolate, and top it with perfect bitterness with bitter chocolate. NOTE I NEVER USE MILK CHOCOLATE AS I DISLIKE IT
 
Alternatives:
Other rich chocolate biscuits would work just fine
Digestive biscuit would also pair well

**Cuisine:** Italian
**Type:** Dinner
**Prep Time:** 15 mins
**Cook Time:** 15 mins
**Spice Level:** 5/5
**Savoury Level:** 5/5
**Salt Level:** 5/5
**Sweetness Level:** 3/5 
**Umami Level:** 5/5 
**Sweetness type:** From the cheese, tomatoes, light cream
**Main Flavour profile:** Rich spicy, Italian flavour

**Ingredients:**
*   Pasta - Spaghetti 
*   Onion: 1.5 piece
*   Broccoli: 5 florets
*   Green Capsicum: ½ piece 
*   Yellow Capsicum: ½ piece 
*   Mozzarella cheese: 4 tbsp 
*   Baby corn: 3 pieces
*   Mushroom: 4 pieces
*   Olives: 3-4 pieces
*   Basil leaves: 4-5 
*   Cherry tomato: 1 piece
*   Garlic Cloves: 4-5 
*   Italian herbs: 4 tsp
*   Olive oil: 2 tbsp
*   Cooking/Light Cream: 200ml
*   White Wine: 1 glass

**Procedure:**
1.  Boil pasta for 8 minutes.
2.  Julienne all vegetables.
3.  Heat olive oil in a pan; add garlic and Italian herbs.
4.  Sauté until garlic is golden.
5.  Add remaining vegetables and sauté until cooked.
6.  Stir in light cream and mozzarella cheese until combined.
7.  Add white wine.
8.  Toss in pasta and mix well.
9.  Add pasta water to adjust consistency.
10. Serve garnished with fresh basil and cherry tomato.

**Notes:** Aim for a saucy, herby finish. Prioritize vegetable volume over pasta. Wine provides necessary sweetness. The cream and cheese should complement, not overpower, the garlic and herbs.

**Alternatives:**
*   **Veggies:** Any variety.
*   **Pasta:** Fettuccine or other types.
*   **Alcohol:** Use vodka or omit entirely.
*   **Protein:** Add baked chicken breast (coated in olive oil and Italian herbs), chopped sausages, or meatballs.

Name:  Non-Stick Pan Lasagna
Cuisine: Italian
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 2 Hours 30 mins
Cook TIme: 45 mins
Spice Level: 4/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 5/5
Sweetness Level: 3/5 
Umami Level: 5/5 
Sweetness type: Cheese sauce
Main Flavour profile: Rich herby, Italian flavour

Ingredients:
Whole wheat flour 2.5 cups
Cheese Slice 3 pieces 
Home made marinara sauce ½ cup
Milk 1 - 1.5 cup
Onion 2 piece
Brocoli 8 Florets
Green Capsicum ½   piece
Yellow Capisum ½ piece 
Spinach leaves 10 pieces
Mozzeralla Cheese 5 tbsp
Baby corn 3 pieces
Corn kernels ¼ cup
Mushroom 5 pieces
Olives 3-4 pieces
Garlic Cloves 4-5 
Paneer 100g
Itallian herbs 4 tps
Olive oil 5 tbsp

Procedure:
Lasagna Sheets:
Take whole wheat, add it oil, hot water and salt 
Knead well and create 5 round balls
Using a rolling pin and create 5 round sheets, roll till they are translucent
Cover them using cloth and let it rest for 2 hours

Cheese Sauce:
Add milk into pan and let it simmer a bit
Add in the whole wheat flour using a whisk mix well till its not lumpy
Add in cheese slice and mix well
Add in italian herbs

Sauted filling:
All veggies and paneer should be finely diced 
In a non stick pan add olive oil, chopped garlic cloves and italian herbs
Saute till garlic is golden
Add in veggies and paneer mix well and saute well

Assembly:
Take a deep cooking pot apply olive oil at the bottom
One scoop of marinara sauce at the bottom
Add in One sheet, on it apply marinara sauce, filling and cheese sauce and sprinkle a little mozzarella
Repeat the step till last sheet, on the last sheet add remaining stuffing, mozzarella and cheese sauce. Here add generous amount of mozzarella cheese
Cover the pan with a transparent lid and cook it on medium heat for 45 mins
After cooking let it cool, sprinkle herbs slice it and serve 

My Notes: This lasagna is heavy, cheesy, spicey, packed with flavour. The paneer brings in the protein, the high amount of veggies helps in filling the tummy, the mozzarella cheese in every slice compliments every flavour. Here the pasta sheet is made without using egg and all purpose flour with an healthier wholewheat flour which also brings natural sweetness in the dish and its not heavy on stomach.
 
Alternatives:
Can add more veggies
Can make square or rectangle pasta sheets
Can use traditional oven in order to cook it traditionally
Ready made marinara sauce instead of homemade to save time, but add salt, herbs to make it more flavourfull
Can use all purpose flour too but prefer whole wheat
Can use chicken/beef instead of paneer
Ready made/ store pasta sheets
Name: One Pot Chole/chickpea Curry
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking chole/chickpeas) + 20 mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from onions
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky, earthy taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Onions 2-3 pieces
Tomatoes 1-2 pieces
Garlic Cloves 6-7
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric ½  teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Black Peppercons ½ teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1.5 teaspoon, Chole Masala 1.5 tsp, Kitchen King Masala 1 tsp)
Dried Red Chilli 4-5 pieces
Bay leaves 2-3 pieces
Tea 1 tsp
Salt 2-3 tsp
Kastoori Methi ½ tablespoon
Oil 3-5 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Soak Chole/chickpeas in water overnight or 8 hours
In pressure cooker ad 3-5 tbsp of oil and let it heat
Then add peppercorns, chopped garlic cloves, dried red chillies, bay leaves, cummin seeds and saute until garlic is brown in color
Add chopped onions (juillien of half moons), now let them saute till they are dark brown
Add in the spices and mix well, the base should look deep dark brown
Then add diced tomatoes and mix will
Add the soaked chickpeas and mix well with the curry base
Add water a little over the point till the chole are submerged
Optional Step: Before adding water boil it with tea and then add the water to the pressure cooker
Let it cook for 5-6 whistles
Add in the crushed kastoori methi and sqeed half a lemon
Serve it


My Notes: The main reason behind adding tea water to cook chole is to give it a high umami taste and black color, but I can achieve it without it. What I essentially do is really caremalize my onions and garlic till they are dark brown and then mix it with the spices. Due to this step the base of the curry already looks brown and the burnt onions give it a great umami and sweet taste to the curry, whilst the garlic balances all the spices. Due to this step one can skip the tea part, although I still add the tea as it elevates the entire dish to a whole different level of spice, umami and savoury flavours.

Alternatives:
No dried red chilli → extra red chilli powder, but add it later so it doesn't burn
No kasuri methi → skip it, nothing replicates it exactly but dried fenugreek seeds (half the quantity) get close
Want it more tangy → extra half lemon or a spoon of tamarind paste with the tomatoes 
Want it creamier → spoon of yoghurt stirred in after the last whistle

Serves: 1

Ingredients:
Rolled Oats 90g
Skimmed Milk 150ml
Unsweetened Coco Powder 1 Tablespoon
Honey 1 Tablespoon
Chopped Almonds 5
Chia Seeds 1 Tablespoon
Pumkin seeds 1 Tablespoon
Chocolate chips 1 tablespoon

Procedure:
Add Milk, Honey and Coco into a nutriblender, Blend till all mixed well 
In a bowl add Rolled oats and mixed milk, top it with chia seeds
Put it in the fridge let it soak overnight or 8 hours
Next day top it up with pumpkin seeds and almonds and chocolate chips

My Notes: I love the Dark chocolatey flavour being dominant, one tablespoon of honey is more that enough for sweetness, the pumpkin seeds bring saltiness to the dish balancing the sweetness from honey and coco. The small amount of chocolate chips contributes to the crunchy factor along with almond. Perfect for a pre-workout meal, I do not like adding banana to the dish as it makes it overtly sweet and the banana is way too dominant. The reason I used skimmed milk/light milk is to save on calories as I dont want to feel bloaty during workout. Also do not prefer walnuts as its bitterness throws the dish off balance.

Alternatives:
Pistachios instead of almonds
Sunflower seeds instead of pumpkin seeds

Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks

Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. Honey 1 Tablespoon
Chopped Almonds 5
Chia Seeds 1 Tablespoon
Pumkin seeds 1 Tablespoon
Chocolate chips 1 tablespoon

Procedure:
Add Milk, Honey and Cinnamon into a nutriblender, Blend till all mixed well 
In a bowl add Rolled oats and mixed milk, top it with chia seeds
Put it in the fridge let it soak overnight or 8 hours
Next day top it up with pumpkin seeds and almonds and chocolate chips

My Notes: I love the cinnamon flavour being dominant, one tablespoon of honey is more that enough for sweetness, the pumpkin seeds bring saltiness to the dish balancing the sweetness from honey and cinnamon. The small amount of chocolate chips contributes to the crunchy factor along with almonds and give a hint of chocolatey taste. Perfect for a pre-workout meal, I do not like adding banana to the dish as it makes it overtly sweet and the banana is way too dominant. The reason I used skimmed milk/light milk is to save on calories as I dont want to feel bloaty during workout. Also do not prefer walnuts as its bitterness throws the dish off balance

Alternatives:
Raisins instead of Chocolatechips
Pistachips instead of almonds
Sunflower seeds instead of pumpkin seeds

Your healthiest breakfast option is the Cinnamon Overnight Oats provided above. It balances complex carbohydrates, healthy fats, and protein while remaining calorie-controlled.

Name:  No Bake Strawberry Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight

Ingredients:
Digestive Biscuit 1 packet
Strawberries 1 pack
Brown sugar 4 tbps
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly also add diced up strawberries, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. The main flavour profile is strawberries, it pairs perfectly with whipping cream and cream cheese. The tine chunks in the main base lifts the dist to a new level. The compote does have added sugar but it is very less than what is usually recommended

Name:  Hummus Sandwich
Cuisine: Sandwich
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 10 mins 
Cook TIme: 15 mins
Spice Level: 3/5
Savoury Level: 4/5
Salt Level: 5/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Hummus and cheese
Main Flavour profile: Earthy, Garlicky, Herby full of veggies
Number of sandwiches: 2

Ingredients:
Sour Dough
My Quick Hummus 5 tbsp
Onion 1 piece
Green Capsicum 1 piece
Tomatoes 1 piece
Boiled Brocoli 2 florets chopped
Lettuce leaves 2 big pieces
Olive oil 3 tbsp mixed with italian herbs
Cheese Slices 2

Procedure:
Chop 4 slices from the sour dough
Light toast them on pan
Spread hummus on all of them, add in cheese slice and veggies
Drissle the olive oil mixed with italian herbs on top before closing sandwich
Close the the sandwich and toast till bread is brown
Serve with side of Hummus

My Notes: Fresh light yet filling sandwich. The hummus as a dip makes the sandwich heavy and filling and also dominates the taste, instead of dipping bread and veggies separately in the hummus I recreate that taste by incorporating it in a sandwich. 
 
Alternatives:
Can add more veggies
Can use chicken breast in the main filling
Can use Traditional hummus recipe 
Can use ready made/store bought hummus
Name: Traditional Hummus
Cuisine: Lebanese
Type: Dip
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking Chole/Chickpeas) + 10 mins
Cook TIme: 5 mins
Spice Level: 2/5
Savoury Level: 4/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 2/5 
Umami Level: 4/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from sesame seeds
Main Flavour profile: Rich Earthy flavour and Garlic dominant

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Garlic Cloves 4-5
Sesme Seeds ½ cup
Salt 
Olive Oil 4-8 tbsp
Lemon ½ 
Red Chilli powder 1 tsp

Procedure:
Tahini:
Pan roast sesame seeds till golden
Add to mixer with 2-4 tbsp of olive oil
Add pinch of salt and blend till creamy

Boil the chole in a pressure cooker
Take the boiled chole and add it into nutri blender
Add in Garlic cloves, 2-4 tbsp olive oid, tahini, pinch of salt
Mix till smooth
Store in jar, drizzle ½ tbsp of olive oil and sprinkle the red chillie

My Notes: This is a traditional hummus recipe which I find slightly time consuming due to the tahini step. Adding in tahini brings out sweetness in the hummus which I dont mind but can do without it. At the same time tahini makes it extremely rich in tast and earthy which I do not mind at all. I add more garlic as I like the dominant taste of it.

Alternatives:
No Tahini then my alternative Hummus recipe
Add in beatroot to make purple hummus, increasing the freshness and giving it a vibrant purple and a natural sweetness 
Add in spinach to make a green hummus
Add in roasted red capsicum to make red/orange hummus

**White Wine Pasta (Vino Vedura)**

**Cuisine:** Italian
**Type:** Dinner
**Prep Time:** 15 mins
**Cook TIme:** 15 mins
**Spice Level:** 5/5
**Savoury Level:** 5/5
**Salt Level:** 5/5
**Sweetness Level:** 3/5 
**Umami Level:** 5/5 
**Sweetness type:** From the cheese ,tomatoes , Light cream
**Main Flavour profile:** Rich Spicy, Italian flavour

**Ingredients:**
*   Pasta - Spaghetti 
*   Onion 1.5 piece
*   Brocoli 5 Florets
*   Green Capsicum ½ piece
*   Yellow Capisum ½ piece 
*   Mozzeralla cheese 4 tbsp 
*   Baby corn 3 pieces
*   Mushroom 4 pieces
*   Olives 3-4 pieces
*   Basil leaves 4-5 
*   Cherry tomato 1 piece
*   Garlic Cloves 4-5 
*   Itallian herbs 4 tps
*   Olive oil 2 tbsp
*   Cooking/Light Cream 200ml
*   White Wine 1 Glass

**Procedure:**
1. Boil Pasta for 8 minutes 
2. The veggies should be Jullien
3. In a pan add olive oil then add garlic, italian herbs
4. Saute till garlic is golden
5. Add the rest veggies saute till cooked
6. Add in the light cream along with mozzarella cheese and mix well
7. Add White Wine
8. Add in the pasta and mix well
9. Add pasta water to balance the dish
10. Serve in a pasta dish garnish with fresh basil and cherry tomato

**My Notes:** I prefer my pasta to be on a saucy side and really herby. I love adding more vegetable that pasta as it increases the volume at the same time brings freshness. The wine brings a unique taste a hint of sweetness that is. The pasta is creamy and cheesy but not too much as the italian herbs and garlic dominate the dish, making it overall well balanced.

**Alternatives:**
*   Can swapp any veggies
*   Can use anyother time of Pasta (Fettucini)
*   Can use Vodka instead of white wine
*   Also can skip alcohol 
*   To add protein bake one chicken brest, the brest should be coated in olive oil and italian herbs, added to the final pasta dish and mixed well
*   Chooped sausages
*   Meat balls

The **White Wine Pasta (Vino Vedura)** uses white wine.

PERSONAL RECIPE:
Hey Granth! It’s so exciting that you’re looking to create something new today! I love the energy you bring to the kitchen. Since we’re starting with a blank slate, I want to make sure we hit that perfect flavor profile you love.

To help me shape this recipe for you, could you answer these two quick questions:

1. Are you leaning towards an **Indian-inspired dish** (like your hearty dals or spicy oats) or an **Italian-style creation** (like your non-stick lasagna) today?
2. Since we'll definitely be loading it up with extra veggies and that signature dark-brown garlic, what kind of **protein or base** are you thinking of using to tie it all together? 

Can't wait to see what we build!

***

**Adjustments for a high-protein diet:**
1. **Increase Protein Portions:** Double the lean meat, poultry, fish, or plant-based protein (tofu, tempeh, lentils).
2. **Swap Grains:** Replace refined carbohydrates with high-protein grains like quinoa or farro.
3. **Add Dairy/Legumes:** Incorporate Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, or extra beans into sauces and sides.
4. **Boost Toppings:** Finish dishes with hemp seeds, chia seeds, or sliced almonds for an extra protein punch.

Name: Dal Tadka
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 30mins
Cook TIme: 15mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Onion 1 piece 
Tomato 1 piece
Green Chilli 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon)
Dried Red chillies 4-5 piece 
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add them into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add tomatoes
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: The amount of garlic is high and is cooked for a longer period as the burnt garlic brings out the smokey flavour of the dal that perfectly pairs with the high amount of spice. The sweetness comes from the onions as I let the carmelise a little longer till they are brown as it brings out the natruat oniony sweetness without the need of adding sugar. I like my dal on the thicker side but not too thick its should be soupy yet slurpy, essentially should be able to bite down on few of the lentils.
Alternatives:
Channa Dal 
Urid Dal
Moong Dal 

Additional Options:
Adding Spinach in the pressure cooker with dal will make a new dish called dal palak

Name:  Dark chocolate paneer Ice cream 
Cuisine: Ice Cream
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 10 mins 
Cook TIme: 8 hours
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5

Main Flavour profile: Rich Chocolatey, Bitter coco taste overall sweet

Ingredients:
Paneer 200g
Milk ¼ cup

Chocolate chips ¼ cup

Procedure:

Pour it in a jar, and top it with chocolate chips
Let it set in the fridge for 8 hours

The coco powder and dark chocolate successfully mask the paneer taste and make it chocolatey, the milk helps in achieving smooth consistency, banger dessert

Alternatives:
Can use Bitter chocolate 75% 
Instead of chocolate chips can add chopped dark chocolate
Can add dry fruits such as almonds

Dark chocolate paneer ice cream.

Name: Dark Chocolate Overnight Oats
Cuisine: Versatile 
Type: Breakfast
Prep Time: 5mins
Cook Time: Overnight/ 8 Hours
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Umami Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 3/5 
Chocolate Bitter Sweetness level: 4/5 
Sweetness Type: Natural and Coco
Main Flavour Profile: Dark Chocolate
Serves: 1

Ingredients:
Rolled Oats 90g
Skimmed Milk 150ml
Unsweetened Coco Powder 1 Tablespoon
Honey 1 Tablespoon
Chopped Almonds 5
Chia Seeds 1 Tablespoon
Pumkin seeds 1 Tablespoon
Chocolate chips 1 tablespoon

Procedure:
Add Milk, Honey and Coco into a nutriblender, Blend till all mixed well 
In a bowl add Rolled oats and mixed milk, top it with chia seeds
Put it in the fridge let it soak overnight or 8 hours
Next day top it up with pumpkin seeds and almonds and chocolate chips

My Notes: I love the Dark chocolatey flavour being dominant, one tablespoon of honey is more that enough for sweetness, the pumpkin seeds bring saltiness to the dish balancing the sweetness from honey and coco. The small amount of chocolate chips contributes to the crunchy factor along with almond. Perfect for a pre-workout meal, I do not like adding banana to the dish as it makes it overtly sweet and the banana is way too dominant. The reason I used skimmed milk/light milk is to save on calories as I dont want to feel bloaty during workout. Also do not prefer walnuts as its bitterness throws the dish off balance.

Alternatives:
Pistachios instead of almonds
Sunflower seeds instead of pumpkin seeds

Name:  No Bake Triple Chocolate Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Chocolate and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich Chocolatey, Bitter coco taste, oreo overall sweet

Ingredients:
Oreo Biscuit 1 packet
75% Bitter Chocolate ¾ Bar
55% Dark Chocolate ¾ Bar
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add melted 55% Dark chocolate and mix well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt the 75% bitter chocolate and let it cool a bit, then add remaining whipping cream and mix well.
Pour the bitter chocolate ganache on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, chocolatey, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. I love bitter chocolate and overall dark chocolates. The main flavour profile is for dark chocolate, the base oreos althoug sweet the biscuit is slightly bitter, then we increase the bitterness of the chocolate in the main filling by adding dark chocolate, and top it with perfect bitterness with bitter chocolate. NOTE I NEVER USE MILK CHOCOLATE AS I DISLIKE IT
 
Alternatives:
Other rich chocolate biscuits would work just fine
Digestive biscuit would also pair well

Name: One Pot Chole/chickpea Curry
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking chole/chickpeas) + 20 mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from onions
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky, earthy taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Onions 2-3 pieces
Tomatoes 1-2 pieces
Garlic Cloves 6-7
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric ½  teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Black Peppercons ½ teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1.5 teaspoon, Chole Masala 1.5 tsp, Kitchen King Masala 1 tsp)
Dried Red Chilli 4-5 pieces
Bay leaves 2-3 pieces
Tea 1 tsp
Salt 2-3 tsp
Kastoori Methi ½ tablespoon
Oil 3-5 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Soak Chole/chickpeas in water overnight or 8 hours
In pressure cooker ad 3-5 tbsp of oil and let it heat
Then add peppercorns, chopped garlic cloves, dried red chillies, bay leaves, cummin seeds and saute until garlic is brown in color
Add chopped onions (juillien of half moons), now let them saute till they are dark brown
Add in the spices and mix well, the base should look deep dark brown
Then add diced tomatoes and mix will
Add the soaked chickpeas and mix well with the curry base
Add water a little over the point till the chole are submerged
Optional Step: Before adding water boil it with tea and then add the water to the pressure cooker
Let it cook for 5-6 whistles
Add in the crushed kastoori methi and sqeed half a lemon
Serve it


My Notes: The main reason behind adding tea water to cook chole is to give it a high umami taste and black color, but I can achieve it without it. What I essentially do is really caremalize my onions and garlic till they are dark brown and then mix it with the spices. Due to this step the base of the curry already looks brown and the burnt onions give it a great umami and sweet taste to the curry, whilst the garlic balances all the spices. Due to this step one can skip the tea part, although I still add the tea as it elevates the entire dish to a whole different level of spice, umami and savoury flavours.

Alternatives:
No dried red chilli → extra red chilli powder, but add it later so it doesn't burn
No kasuri methi → skip it, nothing replicates it exactly but dried fenugreek seeds (half the quantity) get close
Want it more tangy → extra half lemon or a spoon of tamarind paste with the tomatoes 
Want it creamier → spoon of yoghurt stirred in after the last whistle

Store in jar, drizzle ½ tbsp of olive oil

Name: One Pot Chole/chickpea Curry
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking chole/chickpeas) + 20 mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from onions
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky, earthy taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Onions 2-3 pieces
Tomatoes 1-2 pieces
Garlic Cloves 6-7
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric ½  teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Black Peppercons ½ teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1.5 teaspoon, Chole Masala 1.5 tsp, Kitchen King Masala 1 tsp)
Dried Red Chilli 4-5 pieces
Bay leaves 2-3 pieces
Tea 1 tsp
Salt 2-3 tsp
Kastoori Methi ½ tablespoon
Oil 3-5 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Soak Chole/chickpeas in water overnight or 8 hours
In pressure cooker ad 3-5 tbsp of oil and let it heat
Then add peppercorns, chopped garlic cloves, dried red chillies, bay leaves, cummin seeds and saute until garlic is brown in color
Add chopped onions (juillien of half moons), now let them saute till they are dark brown
Add in the spices and mix well, the base should look deep dark brown
Then add diced tomatoes and mix will
Add the soaked chickpeas and mix well with the curry base
Add water a little over the point till the chole are submerged
Optional Step: Before adding water boil it with tea and then add the water to the pressure cooker
Let it cook for 5-6 whistles
Add in the crushed kastoori methi and sqeed half a lemon
Serve it


My Notes: The main reason behind adding tea water to cook chole is to give it a high umami taste and black color, but I can achieve it without it. What I essentially do is really caremalize my onions and garlic till they are dark brown and then mix it with the spices. Due to this step the base of the curry already looks brown and the burnt onions give it a great umami and sweet taste to the curry, whilst the garlic balances all the spices. Due to this step one can skip the tea part, although I still add the tea as it elevates the entire dish to a whole different level of spice, umami and savoury flavours.

Alternatives:
No dried red chilli → extra red chilli powder, but add it later so it doesn't burn
No kasuri methi → skip it, nothing replicates it exactly but dried fenugreek seeds (half the quantity) get close
Want it more tangy → extra half lemon or a spoon of tamarind paste with the tomatoes 
Want it creamier → spoon of yoghurt stirred in after the last whistle

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name:  No Bake Strawberry Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Strawberry and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich, Creamy, fruity, slightly tangy taste overall sweet

Ingredients:
Digestive Biscuit 1 packet
Strawberries 1 pack
Brown sugar 4 tbps
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly also add diced up strawberries, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. The main flavour profile is strawberries, it pairs perfectly with whipping cream and cream cheese. The tine chunks in the main base lifts the dist to a new level. The compote does have added sugar but it is very less than what is usually recommended

Alternatives:
Can use ready made strawberry sauce but wont recommend

***

**Alternatives for strawberry:**
Blueberries, raspberries, or blackberries. Use them in equal quantity for both the filling and the compote.

Name:  Banana Bread
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 20 mins 
Cook TIme: 50 mins
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Banana
Main Flavour profile: Naturally sweet with backend chocolatey taste

Ingredients:
Ripe Bananas 2 pieces
Dark chocolate chunks ¼ cup
Baking soda 1 tsp
Vanilla extract few drops 
Yogurt ⅓ cup
Whole wheat flour 1 cup
Brown sugar 4 tbsp
Almonds 5 chopped 

Procedure:
Mash the ripe bananas thoroughly, add yogurt and vanilla extract and mix well
Add the flour and sugar in the mixture and mix well
Add in dark chocolate chunks
Transfer in cake tinm top it up with more chocolate chunks and almonds
Bake at 180 degrees for 50 mins

My Notes: Natural sweetness from the banana dominates and pairs perfectly dark chocolate as they mitigare the overt sweetness. The yogurt makes the cake super moist and fluffym and it is low sugar and healthy as it uses whole wheat and not all purpose flour.

Alternatives:
Can add more dry fruits

**Name:** White Wine Pasta (Vino Vedura) 
**Cuisine:** Italian
**Type:** Dinner
**Prep Time:** 15 mins
**Cook Time:** 15 mins
**Spice Level:** 5/5
**Savoury Level:** 5/5
**Salt Level:** 5/5
**Sweetness Level:** 3/5 
**Umami Level:** 5/5 
**Sweetness type:** From the cheese, tomatoes, light cream
**Main Flavour profile:** Rich Spicy, Italian flavour

**Ingredients:**
* Pasta - Spaghetti 
* Onion: 1.5 pieces
* Broccoli: 5 florets
* Green Capsicum: ½ piece 
* Yellow Capsicum: ½ piece 
* Mozzarella cheese: 4 tbsp 
* Baby corn: 3 pieces
* Mushroom: 4 pieces
* Olives: 3-4 pieces
* Basil leaves: 4-5 
* Cherry tomato: 1 piece
* Garlic cloves: 4-5 
* Italian herbs: 4 tsp
* Olive oil: 2 tbsp
* Cooking/Light Cream: 200ml
* White Wine: 1 glass

**Procedure:**
1. Boil pasta for 8 minutes. 
2. Julienne the vegetables. 
3. In a pan, heat olive oil, then add garlic and Italian herbs. 
4. Sauté until garlic is golden. 
5. Add the remaining vegetables and sauté until cooked. 
6. Add the light cream and mozzarella cheese; mix well. 
7. Deglaze with white wine. 
8. Add the pasta and toss to coat. 
9. Use pasta water to adjust sauce consistency. 
10. Serve in a pasta dish; garnish with fresh basil and cherry tomato.

**Notes:** Preference for saucy, herb-forward pasta. High vegetable-to-pasta ratio. Wine provides a subtle sweetness. Balance achieved through dominant garlic and herbs against the cream and cheese.

**Alternatives:** 
* Veggies: Fully interchangeable. 
* Pasta: Swap for Fettuccine. 
* Alcohol: Use Vodka or omit entirely. 
* Protein: Add baked chicken breast (coated in olive oil and herbs), chopped sausages, or meatballs.

**Recipe:** Granth’s Hummus Chicken Melt Wrap

**Ingredients:**
* Helga's wholemeal wraps
* Deli Originals traditional style hommus
* Woolworths RSPCA approved chicken breast fillets
* Red onions
* Cheese slices
* Iceberg lettuce

**Method:**
1. **Sear:** Thinly slice chicken and red onion. Pan-fry over high heat until chicken is cooked through and onion is charred.
2. **Melt:** Place cheese slices over the hot chicken and onion in the pan until melted.
3. **Assemble:** Spread hommus onto the wrap. Add the chicken, onion, and cheese mixture. 
4. **Finish:** Top with shredded iceberg lettuce, roll tightly, and serve.

Done.

*   Woolworths RSPCA approved chicken breast fillets
*   Red onions
*   Cheese slices
*   Iceberg lettuce

**Method:**
1. **Sear:** Thinly slice the chicken breast and red onions. Sear in a hot non-stick pan until the chicken is golden and the onions are slightly charred (for that signature Granth-style spice/umami).
2. **Layer:** Lay out the wholemeal wrap. Spread a generous base of hommus.
3. **Melt:** Place the cheese slice directly onto the hot chicken in the pan for 30 seconds until just melting, then transfer to the wrap.
4. **Assemble:** Top with fresh, cold iceberg lettuce for crunch. 
5. **Toast:** Fold the wrap tightly and sear both sides in the same pan until crispy. Serve immediately.

**Inspired by:** Hummus Wraps, Non-Stick Pan Lasagna
Name:  Hummus Wraps 
Cuisine: Wrap

Ingredients:
Multigrain Tortilla Wrap

Garlic Cloves 3-4

Yogurt ½ cup

Italian Herbs
My Quick Hummus 3 tbsp
Potato 1

Procedure:
In a bown add yogurt, garlic, lemon, italian herbs and pepper mix well
Then add chopped chicken breast and let it marrinate for 20 mins
Chop Potato into wedges, add little oil, salt and italian herbs and bake them in oven for 20 mins at 200 degrees
Add the chicken marinate in a pan cook till all pieces are brown and cooked
Light toast tortilla wrap, then apply hummus
Add lettuce leaf, cube cut veggies, chicken pieces and potato wedges
Wrap it up and toast it for little longer till the wrap is brown and toasty
Serve with remaining Wedges 

My Notes: The wrap is filling due to chicken and potatoes, it is earthy and herby. The veggies makes it supper fresh especially the lettuce

Name:  Non-Stick Pan Lasagna
Cuisine: Italian
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 2 Hours 30 mins
Cook TIme: 45 mins
Spice Level: 4/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 5/5
Sweetness Level: 3/5 
Umami Level: 5/5 
Sweetness type: Cheese sauce
Main Flavour profile: Rich herby, Italian flavour

Ingredients:
Whole wheat flour 2.5 cups
Cheese Slice 3 pieces 
Home made marinara sauce ½ cup
Milk 1 - 1.5 cup
Onion 2 piece
Brocoli 8 Florets
Green Capsicum ½   piece
Yellow Capisum ½ piece 
Spinach leaves 10 pieces
Mozzeralla Cheese 5 tbsp
Baby corn 3 pieces
Corn kernels ¼ cup
Mushroom 5 pieces
Olives 3-4 pieces
Garlic Cloves 4-5 
Paneer 100g
Itallian herbs 4 tps
Olive oil 5 tbsp

Procedure:
Lasagna Sheets:
Take whole wheat, add it oil, hot water and salt 
Knead well and create 5 round balls
Using a rolling pin and create 5 round sheets, roll till they are translucent
Cover them using cloth and let it rest for 2 hours

Cheese Sauce:
Add milk into pan and let it simmer a bit
Add in the whole wheat flour using a whisk mix well till its not lumpy
Add in cheese slice and mix well
Add in italian herbs

Sauted filling:
All veggies and paneer should be finely diced 
In a non stick pan add olive oil, chopped garlic cloves and italian herbs
Saute till garlic is golden
Add in veggies and paneer mix well and saute well

Assembly:
Take a deep cooking pot apply olive oil at the bottom
One scoop of marinara sauce at the bottom
Add in One sheet, on it apply marinara sauce, filling and cheese sauce and sprinkle a little mozzarella
Repeat the step till last sheet, on the last sheet add remaining stuffing, mozzarella and cheese sauce. Here add generous amount of mozzarella cheese
Cover the pan with a transparent lid and cook it on medium heat for 45 mins
After cooking let it cool, sprinkle herbs slice it and serve 

My Notes: This lasagna is heavy, cheesy, spicey, packed with flavour. The paneer brings in the protein, the high amount of veggies helps in filling the tummy, the mozzarella cheese in every slice compliments every flavour. Here the pasta sheet is made without using egg and all purpose flour with an healthier wholewheat flour which also brings natural sweetness in the dish and its not heavy on stomach.
 
Alternatives:
Can add more veggies
Can make square or rectangle pasta sheets
Can use traditional oven in order to cook it traditionally
Ready made marinara sauce instead of homemade to save time, but add salt, herbs to make it more flavourfull
Can use all purpose flour too but prefer whole wheat
Can use chicken/beef instead of paneer
Ready made/ store pasta sheets

**Recipe:** Quick-Boost Chocolate Oats
**Cuisine:** Versatile
**Time:** 5 mins prep (instant)
**Spice:** 0/5

**Ingredients:**
* Rolled Oats: 60g
* Full Cream Fresh Milk: 150ml
* Nesquik Chocolate Powder: 2 tablespoons

**Method:**

Name: Dark Chocolate Overnight Oats
Cuisine: Versatile 
Type: Breakfast
Prep Time: 5mins
Cook Time: Overnight/ 8 Hours
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Umami Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 3/5 
Chocolate Bitter Sweetness level: 4/5 
Sweetness Type: Natural and Coco
Main Flavour Profile: Dark Chocolate
Serves: 1

Ingredients:
Rolled Oats 90g
Skimmed Milk 150ml
Unsweetened Coco Powder 1 Tablespoon
Honey 1 Tablespoon
Chopped Almonds 5
Chia Seeds 1 Tablespoon
Pumkin seeds 1 Tablespoon
Chocolate chips 1 tablespoon

Procedure:
Add Milk, Honey and Coco into a nutriblender, Blend till all mixed well 
In a bowl add Rolled oats and mixed milk, top it with chia seeds
Put it in the fridge let it soak overnight or 8 hours
Next day top it up with pumpkin seeds and almonds and chocolate chips

My Notes: I love the Dark chocolatey flavour being dominant, one tablespoon of honey is more that enough for sweetness, the pumpkin seeds bring saltiness to the dish balancing the sweetness from honey and coco. The small amount of chocolate chips contributes to the crunchy factor along with almond. Perfect for a pre-workout meal, I do not like adding banana to the dish as it makes it overtly sweet and the banana is way too dominant. The reason I used skimmed milk/light milk is to save on calories as I dont want to feel bloaty during workout. Also do not prefer walnuts as its bitterness throws the dish off balance.

Alternatives:
Pistachios instead of almonds
Sunflower seeds instead of pumpkin seeds

Name:  No Bake Strawberry Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Strawberry and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich, Creamy, fruity, slightly tangy taste overall sweet

Ingredients:
Digestive Biscuit 1 packet
Strawberries 1 pack
Brown sugar 4 tbps
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly also add diced up strawberries, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. The main flavour profile is strawberries, it pairs perfectly with whipping cream and cream cheese. The tine chunks in the main base lifts the dist to a new level. The compote does have added sugar but it is very less than what is usually recommended

Alternatives:
Can use ready made strawberry sauce but wont recommend
Name:  No Bake Triple Chocolate Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Chocolate and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich Chocolatey, Bitter coco taste, oreo overall sweet

Ingredients:
Oreo Biscuit 1 packet
75% Bitter Chocolate ¾ Bar
55% Dark Chocolate ¾ Bar
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add melted 55% Dark chocolate and mix well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt the 75% bitter chocolate and let it cool a bit, then add remaining whipping cream and mix well.
Pour the bitter chocolate ganache on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, chocolatey, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. I love bitter chocolate and overall dark chocolates. The main flavour profile is for dark chocolate, the base oreos althoug sweet the biscuit is slightly bitter, then we increase the bitterness of the chocolate in the main filling by adding dark chocolate, and top it with perfect bitterness with bitter chocolate. NOTE I NEVER USE MILK CHOCOLATE AS I DISLIKE IT
 
Alternatives:
Other rich chocolate biscuits would work just fine
Digestive biscuit would also pair well

Hello Granth! It is so wonderful to see you in the kitchen today! Using sourdough and hummus as our foundation is a fantastic starting point—it sounds like we can create something truly vibrant and satisfying.

To make sure we get this just right for your palate, I have two quick questions:

1. **For the base:** Are you feeling like a toasted open-faced sandwich topped with a heavy mix of sautéed vegetables (which we can caramelize to your preference), or are you leaning toward a Mediterranean-Indian fusion plate where we incorporate your signature dark-brown onions and extra garlic into the hummus itself?

2. **The "Finish":** Since we are using hummus, are you in the mood to keep it fresh with a heavy hand of lemon juice and a garnish of cherry tomatoes and basil, or would you like to incorporate a spicy yogurt drizzle to bring that signature 4/5 spice level and cooling balance?

Let me know what you're craving, and we'll get cooking!

***

**Comparison:**
Recipe 1 is a vegetable-topped sourdough toast with fresh, acidic garnishes. Recipe 2 is a fusion hummus bowl featuring cooked aromatics and a spicy yogurt element.

Name:  Hummus Sandwich
Cuisine: Sandwich
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 10 mins 
Cook TIme: 15 mins
Spice Level: 3/5
Savoury Level: 4/5
Salt Level: 5/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Hummus and cheese
Main Flavour profile: Earthy, Garlicky, Herby full of veggies
Number of sandwiches: 2

Ingredients:
Sour Dough
My Quick Hummus 5 tbsp
Onion 1 piece
Green Capsicum 1 piece
Tomatoes 1 piece
Boiled Brocoli 2 florets chopped
Lettuce leaves 2 big pieces
Olive oil 3 tbsp mixed with italian herbs
Cheese Slices 2

Procedure:
Chop 4 slices from the sour dough
Light toast them on pan
Spread hummus on all of them, add in cheese slice and veggies
Drissle the olive oil mixed with italian herbs on top before closing sandwich
Close the the sandwich and toast till bread is brown
Serve with side of Hummus

My Notes: Fresh light yet filling sandwich. The hummus as a dip makes the sandwich heavy and filling and also dominates the taste, instead of dipping bread and veggies separately in the hummus I recreate that taste by incorporating it in a sandwich. 
 
Alternatives:
Can add more veggies
Can use chicken breast in the main filling
Can use Traditional hummus recipe 
Can use ready made/store bought hummus
Name: Traditional Hummus
Cuisine: Lebanese
Type: Dip
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking Chole/Chickpeas) + 10 mins
Cook TIme: 5 mins
Spice Level: 2/5
Savoury Level: 4/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 2/5 
Umami Level: 4/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from sesame seeds
Main Flavour profile: Rich Earthy flavour and Garlic dominant

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Garlic Cloves 4-5
Sesme Seeds ½ cup
Salt 
Olive Oil 4-8 tbsp
Lemon ½ 
Red Chilli powder 1 tsp

Procedure:
Tahini:
Pan roast sesame seeds till golden
Add to mixer with 2-4 tbsp of olive oil
Add pinch of salt and blend till creamy

Boil the chole in a pressure cooker
Take the boiled chole and add it into nutri blender
Add in Garlic cloves, 2-4 tbsp olive oid, tahini, pinch of salt
Mix till smooth
Store in jar, drizzle ½ tbsp of olive oil and sprinkle the red chillie

My Notes: This is a traditional hummus recipe which I find slightly time consuming due to the tahini step. Adding in tahini brings out sweetness in the hummus which I dont mind but can do without it. At the same time tahini makes it extremely rich in tast and earthy which I do not mind at all. I add more garlic as I like the dominant taste of it.

Alternatives:
No Tahini then my alternative Hummus recipe
Add in beatroot to make purple hummus, increasing the freshness and giving it a vibrant purple and a natural sweetness 
Add in spinach to make a green hummus
Add in roasted red capsicum to make red/orange hummus

Name: Dal Tadka
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 30mins
Cook TIme: 15mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Onion 1 piece 
Tomato 1 piece
Green Chilli 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon)
Dried Red chillies 4-5 piece 
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add them into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add tomatoes
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: The amount of garlic is high and is cooked for a longer period as the burnt garlic brings out the smokey flavour of the dal that perfectly pairs with the high amount of spice. The sweetness comes from the onions as I let the carmelise a little longer till they are brown as it brings out the natruat oniony sweetness without the need of adding sugar. I like my dal on the thicker side but not too thick its should be soupy yet slurpy, essentially should be able to bite down on few of the lentils.
Alternatives:
Channa Dal 
Urid Dal
Moong Dal 

Additional Options:
Adding Spinach in the pressure cooker with dal will make a new dish called dal palak

Name: Dark Chocolate Overnight Oats
Cuisine: Versatile 
Type: Breakfast
Prep Time: 5mins
Cook Time: Overnight/ 8 Hours
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Umami Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 3/5 
Chocolate Bitter Sweetness level: 4/5 
Sweetness Type: Natural and Coco
Main Flavour Profile: Dark Chocolate
Serves: 1

Ingredients:
Rolled Oats 90g
Skimmed Milk 150ml
Unsweetened Coco Powder 1 Tablespoon
Honey 1 Tablespoon
Chopped Almonds 5
Chia Seeds 1 Tablespoon
Pumkin seeds 1 Tablespoon
Chocolate chips 1 tablespoon

Procedure:
Add Milk, Honey and Coco into a nutriblender, Blend till all mixed well 
In a bowl add Rolled oats and mixed milk, top it with chia seeds
Put it in the fridge let it soak overnight or 8 hours
Next day top it up with pumpkin seeds and almonds and chocolate chips

My Notes: I love the Dark chocolatey flavour being dominant, one tablespoon of honey is more that enough for sweetness, the pumpkin seeds bring saltiness to the dish balancing the sweetness from honey and coco. The small amount of chocolate chips contributes to the crunchy factor along with almond. Perfect for a pre-workout meal, I do not like adding banana to the dish as it makes it overtly sweet and the banana is way too dominant. The reason I used skimmed milk/light milk is to save on calories as I dont want to feel bloaty during workout. Also do not prefer walnuts as its bitterness throws the dish off balance.

Sunflower seeds instead of pumpkin seeds

Name:  No Bake Triple Chocolate Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 0/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Chocolate and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich Chocolatey, Bitter coco taste, oreo overall sweet

Ingredients:
Oreo Biscuit 1 packet
75% Bitter Chocolate ¾ Bar
55% Dark Chocolate ¾ Bar
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add melted 55% Dark chocolate and mix well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt the 75% bitter chocolate and let it cool a bit, then add remaining whipping cream and mix well.
Pour the bitter chocolate ganache on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, chocolatey, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. I love bitter chocolate and overall dark chocolates. The main flavour profile is for dark chocolate, the base oreos althoug sweet the biscuit is slightly bitter, then we increase the bitterness of the chocolate in the main filling by adding dark chocolate, and top it with perfect bitterness with bitter chocolate. NOTE I NEVER USE MILK CHOCOLATE AS I DISLIKE IT
 
Alternatives:
Other rich chocolate biscuits would work just fine
Digestive biscuit would also pair well

Name: One Pot Chole/chickpea Curry
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 8 hours (soaking chole/chickpeas) + 20 mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5 
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from onions
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky, earthy taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Chole/Chickpeas 1 Cup
Onions 2-3 pieces
Tomatoes 1-2 pieces
Garlic Cloves 6-7
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric ½  teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Black Peppercons ½ teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1.5 teaspoon, Chole Masala 1.5 tsp, Kitchen King Masala 1 tsp)
Dried Red Chilli 4-5 pieces
Bay leaves 2-3 pieces
Tea 1 tsp
Salt 2-3 tsp
Kastoori Methi ½ tablespoon
Oil 3-5 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Soak Chole/chickpeas in water overnight or 8 hours
In pressure cooker ad 3-5 tbsp of oil and let it heat
Then add peppercorns, chopped garlic cloves, dried red chillies, bay leaves, cummin seeds and saute until garlic is brown in color
Add chopped onions (juillien of half moons), now let them saute till they are dark brown
Add in the spices and mix well, the base should look deep dark brown
Then add diced tomatoes and mix will
Add the soaked chickpeas and mix well with the curry base
Add water a little over the point till the chole are submerged
Optional Step: Before adding water boil it with tea and then add the water to the pressure cooker
Let it cook for 5-6 whistles
Add in the crushed kastoori methi and sqeed half a lemon
Serve it


My Notes: The main reason behind adding tea water to cook chole is to give it a high umami taste and black color, but I can achieve it without it. What I essentially do is really caremalize my onions and garlic till they are dark brown and then mix it with the spices. Due to this step the base of the curry already looks brown and the burnt onions give it a great umami and sweet taste to the curry, whilst the garlic balances all the spices. Due to this step one can skip the tea part, although I still add the tea as it elevates the entire dish to a whole different level of spice, umami and savoury flavours.

Alternatives:
No dried red chilli → extra red chilli powder, but add it later so it doesn't burn
No kasuri methi → skip it, nothing replicates it exactly but dried fenugreek seeds (half the quantity) get close
Want it more tangy → extra half lemon or a spoon of tamarind paste with the tomatoes 
Want it creamier → spoon of yoghurt stirred in after the last whistle

Garlic Cloves 4-5
Oregano 1-1.5 tbsp
Chilli Flakes 1-1.5 tbsp
Salt 
Olive Oil 4-8 tbsp
Lemon ½ 

Procedure:
Boil the chole in a pressure cooker
Take the boiled chole and add it into nutri blender
Add in Garlic cloves, 2-4 tbsp olive oid, oregano and chilli flakes, pinch of salt
Might need to add water 2-4 tbsp 
Mix till smooth
Store in jar, drizzle ½ tbsp of olive oil 

My Notes: This is my quick hummus, infact preferred hummus recipe as it is more savoury and garlic, olive oil naturally shine through. Adding in the oregano can make it more italian but as a matter of fact the earth taste of chickpeas and garlic is so strong that the oregano does nothing but rather just alleviates their flavour. The only possible flag is that the creaminess is not as good as traditional hummus so to counter that I add in a little water to make it more smoother. The Chilli flakes help in additional spice

Alternatives:
Add Chopped Olives to make it richer

Your Dal Tadka spice level is 5/5.

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name: Dal Tadka
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Lunch/Dinner
Prep Time: 30mins
Cook TIme: 15mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Onion 1 piece 
Tomato 1 piece
Green Chilli 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon)
Dried Red chillies 4-5 piece 
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add them into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add tomatoes
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: The amount of garlic is high and is cooked for a longer period as the burnt garlic brings out the smokey flavour of the dal that perfectly pairs with the high amount of spice. The sweetness comes from the onions as I let the carmelise a little longer till they are brown as it brings out the natruat oniony sweetness without the need of adding sugar. I like my dal on the thicker side but not too thick its should be soupy yet slurpy, essentially should be able to bite down on few of the lentils.
Alternatives:
Channa Dal 
Urid Dal
Moong Dal 

Additional Options:
Adding Spinach in the pressure cooker with dal will make a new dish called dal palak

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name: Masala Khicdi 
Cuisine: Indian
Type: Dinner
Prep Time: 20mins
Cook TIme: 25mins
Spice Level: 5/5
Savoury Level: 5/5
Salt Level: 4/5
Sweetness Level: 1/5
Umami Level: 3/5 
Sweetness type: Natural from veggies
Main Flavour profile: Packed with indian spices and garlicky taste
Serves: 3

Ingredients:
Toor Dal/split pigeon pea ½ cup
Masoor Dal/red lentils ½ cup
Rice 1 Cup
Onion 1 to 1.5 piece 
Tomato 1 to 1.5 piece
Capsicum ½ piece
Carrot ½ piece
Peas ¼ cup
Green beans ¼ cup
Potatoes 1
Corn ¼ cup
Green Chillie 1-2 piece
Garlic cloves 6 pieces
Half Lemon juice
Indian Spices (Red Chilli powder 2 teaspoon, turmeric 1 teaspoon, Cummin seeds ½  teaspoon, Coriander Powder 2 teaspoons, Garam Masala ¼ teaspoon, Sunday Masala 1 teaspoon, Khichdi Masala 1 tsp)
Salt 2 to 2.5 teaspoon
Oil 2-2.5 tablespoon

Procedure:
Onions should be finely diced cut
Carots, green beans, potatoes can be diced cut or kept long
Soak both daal in water for 15-20 mins
Add dal and rice together into a pressure cooker, add 2 tps of salt, ½ teaspoon of turmeric powder and corn
Cook them on high heat for 4 whistles
Tadka Recipie
Onions, Garlic and Tomatoes should be diced and finely cut
Add Oil to the pan, let it heat
Add Chopped garlic cloves, green chilli and Cummin seeds, Dried chilli
Let the Gloves get slightly brown to brown
Add Onions, saute until they are slightly brown to brown
Add in the remaining spices and ½  tsp of salt
Saute and mix well, then add rest of the vegies
Cookwell until mixed well
Then add the tadka to the cooked dal and rice in pressure cooker, mix well
Add water according to preference 
Serve in a bowl, garnish with coriander and sprinkle lime juice on top

My Notes: I like to add more garlic to cook it a little longer than required to enure its taste is prevalent in the dish as a lot of different spices are being added. The green chilli helps in giving the dish natural spice. I love adding more vegetables in every dish of mine, the make the dish very fresh and fibrous. The sweetness or hint of sweetness comes from carrots, peas and potatoes which is more than enough for this dish, the carmelised onions also add to the sweetness. Also I like my khichdi being soft and mushy.

Alternatives:
In vegetables anything can be used but onion tomatoes and potatoes are a must
Green Moong Dal instead of toor dal

Additional Options:
Spinach 
Cauliflower 
For more protein add in boiled soya chunks about ¼ cup

**PERSONAL RECIPE:**
Name:  No Bake Strawberry Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Strawberry and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Rich, Creamy, fruity, slightly tangy taste overall sweet

Ingredients:
Digestive Biscuit 1 packet
Strawberries 1 pack
Brown sugar 4 tbps
Whipping Cream ½  cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly also add diced up strawberries, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Make Strawberry compote using strawberries and brown sugar
Pour the compote on top 
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, fruity, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness.. The main flavour profile is strawberries, it pairs perfectly with whipping cream and cream cheese. The tine chunks in the main base lifts the dist to a new level. The compote does have added sugar but it is very less than what is usually recommended

Alternatives:
Can use ready made strawberry sauce but wont recommend

***

**Alternatives for strawberry:**
Blueberries, raspberries, or blackberries. Use similar weights and adjust the sugar in the compote to account for varying natural acidity.

Can add dry fruits such as raisins, almonds, cashews and pistachios

Name:  No Bake Biscoff Cheese Cake
Cuisine: Cakes
Type: Dessert 
Prep Time: 30 mins 
Cook TIme: Overnight
Spice Level: 0/5
Savoury Level: 0/5
Salt Level: 1/5
Sweetness Level: 5/5 
Umami Level: 0/5 
Sweetness type: Biscoff and Whipping cream
Main Flavour profile: Sweet, slight saltiness, biscoff

Ingredients:
Biscoff Biscuit 1 packet
Biscoff Spread ½ Jar
Whipping Cream ¼ cup
Cream cheese 250g
Butter 7 tbps

Procedure:
Crumble the biscuit into a blender till its is fine 
Add it into a bowl and add melted butter
Add the biscuit into a cake tin and press it hard using a spoon to make it a solid base. Refrigerate for 15 mins
In a different bowl empty creamcheese and soften it a bit, add Biscoff spread and mix it well
In a separate bowl beat the whipping cream to medium-high peaks
Add the whipping cream to the creamcheese base and fold it slowly, mix well
Transfer the filling to the cake tin and let it set for 1 hour
Melt remaining biscoff spread and pour it over the filling
Now let the entire cake set overnight

My Notes: Creamy, sweet, cheese, dense and rich in flavour. The best part is that it as no extra added sugar as I like my desserts to be at the lower end of sweetness. Biscoff biscuit gives out rich carmelised butter taste the crunchy base that is, the center filling is smooth, creamy and sweet topped with pure melted biscoff spread creating the perfect dessert.
 
Alternatives:
None, the recipe is full proof
